levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the interface description for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.
The module identifies the principal physical, electrical, thermal, control, monitoring, maintenance, and ground support interfaces associated with ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Interface Scope
levelledPara.para: The interface scope includes the controlled boundaries between the hybrid-electric architecture and adjacent aircraft systems, subsystems, support equipment, diagnostic functions, and publication artefacts.

This data module is descriptive only. It does not define detailed interface control drawings, wiring lists, connector pin allocations, software interface control documents, qualification procedures, or maintenance tasks. Those items shall be controlled by the applicable engineering, interface control, procedural, test, and fault isolation data modules.

levelledPara.title: Principal Interface Categories
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture has the following principal interface categories:

listItem.para: Energy source and energy conversion interfaces.
Electrical power distribution and protection interfaces.
Thermal management and heat rejection interfaces.
Control, monitoring, indication, and recording interfaces.
Installation, mounting, access, and structural support interfaces.

levelledPara.title: Aircraft-Level Propulsion Command Interface
levelledPara.para: The aircraft-level propulsion command interface transfers commanded propulsion demand, operating mode requests, inhibit conditions, availability status, and protective limitation signals between the aircraft control functions and the hybrid-electric architecture.
Command validity, authority allocation, failure response, and transition logic shall be defined by the approved aircraft control and propulsion control baseline.

levelledPara.title: Energy Source and Conversion Interface
levelledPara.para: The energy source and conversion interface connects the hybrid-electric architecture to the applicable energy storage, conversion, conditioning, buffering, and isolation equipment.
This interface includes readiness status, power availability, energy flow limits, protective trip status, isolation state, configuration status, and maintenance reporting provisions.

levelledPara.title: Electrical Power Distribution Interface
levelledPara.para: The electrical power distribution interface routes controlled electrical power between energy conversion equipment, propulsion drive equipment, auxiliary hybrid-electric loads, protection equipment, and aircraft-level electrical systems.
Interface control shall include voltage class, current limits, switching logic, protection coordination, fault containment, isolation state, and applicable electrical safety provisions.

levelledPara.title: Propulsion Drive Interface
levelledPara.para: The propulsion drive interface connects the power distribution layer to the motor drive equipment, electric machines, local sensors, protection devices, cooling provisions, and propulsion control functions.

This interface supports commanded torque or power delivery, motor status reporting, drive limitation, abnormal condition reporting, and controlled transition to limited, degraded, or safe-state operation.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Management Interface
levelledPara.para: The thermal management interface transfers thermal loads, temperature data, coolant or heat-transfer status, heat rejection capacity, operating limits, and degradation information between hybrid-electric equipment and the aircraft thermal management functions.
Thermal interface control shall support equipment protection, availability management, operating mode limitation, and maintenance reporting when thermal capacity is reduced or exceeded.

levelledPara.title: Control and Monitoring Interface
levelledPara.para: The control and monitoring interface exchanges operating state, command validation, sensor data, equipment status, protection status, configuration data, and health-monitoring data between the hybrid-electric architecture and aircraft-level control and monitoring systems.
The interface shall preserve deterministic state reporting, unambiguous mode indication, controlled fault propagation, and traceable diagnostic event recording.

levelledPara.title: Cockpit Indication and Recording Interface
levelledPara.para: The cockpit indication and recording interface provides the flight crew and recording systems with controlled information related to hybrid-electric system availability, operating mode, limitation state, abnormal condition status, and applicable advisory or warning data.
The detailed display logic, alerting logic, crew action logic, and recording requirements shall be controlled by the applicable aircraft indication, recording, operations, and certification baseline.

levelledPara.title: Central Maintenance Interface
levelledPara.para: The central maintenance interface transfers fault messages, built-in-test results, configuration status, maintenance event data, diagnostic records, and corrective-action references between the hybrid-electric architecture and the aircraft maintenance systems.
Fault code mapping, maintenance message priority, dispatch effect, and return-to-service logic shall be defined in the applicable fault isolation, maintenance, and operational data modules.

levelledPara.title: Ground Support Interface
levelledPara.para: The ground support interface provides controlled access for maintenance diagnostics, software or configuration checks, test initiation, data download, and service support activities.
Ground support access shall preserve electrical safety, system isolation, configuration control, cyber/data integrity, and maintenance traceability.

levelledPara.title: Installation and Structural Interface
levelledPara.para: The installation and structural interface includes mounting points, equipment access zones, cable and conduit routing, cooling interface provisions, equipment clearance, maintainability access, and aircraft-level installation constraints.
Structural load paths, vibration limits, access requirements, and environmental constraints shall be controlled by the applicable installation, structures, and equipment qualification baselines.

levelledPara.title: Safety and Isolation Interface
levelledPara.para: The safety and isolation interface supports controlled isolation, interlock status, protective shutdown, fault containment, inhibit logic, safe-state transition, and maintenance safety provisions.
Isolation states and protective responses shall be validated against the approved safety assessment, certification basis, maintenance procedures, and applicable operational constraints.

levelledPara.title: Interface Control Philosophy
levelledPara.para: Each interface shall have a controlled source, destination, function, signal or power class, operating state dependency, failure effect, maintenance implication, and traceability reference.

Interface changes shall be configuration-controlled and assessed for impact on safety, certification, maintainability, S1000D publication content, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Interface Boundaries
levelledPara.para: This data module describes interface boundaries at architecture level. It does not allocate final connector identifiers, wire numbers, software message identifiers, installation drawing references, or test limits unless those values are provided by an approved engineering baseline.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module supports deterministic traceability between architecture-level interface definition, S1000D CSDB descriptive content, interface control records, maintenance reporting logic, safety assessment evidence, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, interface control baseline, programme configuration baseline, safety assessment evidence, and verification records before CSDB release.
